data.text/markdown:
Curiosity Killed the Snake: Re-evaluating Intrinsic Motivation

An interactive exploration of "Curiosity-driven Exploration by Self-supervised Prediction" (Pathak et al., 2017).

The Problem: Sparse Reward Trap

In Deep Reinforcement Learning, an agent learns by maximizing a reward signal. But what happens when the environment is vast, the board is empty, and the reward is incredibly hard to find by pure chance? When an ||(\epsilon||)-greedy agent (like a standard DQN) faces a sparse environment, its exploration is entirely random. It suffers from "Catastrophic Amnesia" of its early states, spinning in circles rather than systematically mapping the environment. Run the baseline agent below to see how it performs when the reward is 10 steps away.

data.text/html:
Curiosity Killed the Snake: Re-evaluating Intrinsic Motivation

An interactive exploration of "Curiosity-driven Exploration by Self-supervised Prediction" (Pathak et al., 2017).

Act 1: The Sparse Reward Trap

In traditional Deep Reinforcement Learning, an agent learns by maximizing a reward signal. But what happens when the environment is vast, the board is empty, and the reward is incredibly hard to find by pure chance? When an ||(\epsilon||)-greedy agent (like a standard DQN) faces a sparse environment, its exploration is entirely random. It suffers from "Catastrophic Amnesia" of its early states, spinning in circles rather than systematically mapping the environment. Deploy the baseline agent below to see how it performs when the reward is 10 steps away.
